title: Even More Global Grain Exports Now Expected
section_path: Even More Global Grain Exports Now Expected
section_path_text: Even More Global Grain Exports Now Expected
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly Executive Report, the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has released its latest forecast for the upcoming 2025/26 grain trade season and is now predicting that global coarse grain, wheat, soybean, and soymeal exports will collectively total 729.4 million tons. This is 3.7 million tons (1%) more than was predicted a month ago and would mark a year-on-year increase of 21.4 million tons (3%). Remaining very significant, too, is that during the 2024/25 global grain season, a year-on-year contraction of approximately 2.4 million tons occurred.
